How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways How amphetamine use may be affecting our waterways Amphetamine use contributes to waterway contamination primarily through improper disposal of unused medications and through human and animal excretion. Unused amphetamines that are flushed down toilets or discarded into household drains can enter sewage systems and eventually reach water treatment facilities that may not be fully equipped to remove these substances. As a result, trace amounts of amphetamines can find their way into treated wastewater and then into nearby waterways. Additionally, humans and animals that have used or been exposed to amphetamines excrete these drugs and their metabolites, which can also enter water bodies through waste discharge.
